_doc.what: Durable specialist DEFINITIONS, not resident processes. A desk = this roster entry + a boot pack (boot-packs.template.yaml) + the accumulated conclusions your ledger keeps for its area + the summon contract (summon-protocol.md). Summon by default; stand a warm desk only when traffic justifies it (standing-desk-lifecycle.md).
_doc.scope: areas is the prose boundary; paths are the repo prefixes the desk owns. Wake routing (wake-routing.md) and registry review both key off paths.
_doc.posture: read-only is the default for every desk. A boot pack never grants writes; only the summoning task spec does, explicitly, naming the paths the grant covers.
_doc.fare_classes: How urgent the call is and what that changes. routine = batched background work, run it async. priority = the caller is blocked, run it synchronously. emergency = live incident, interrupt semantics, and your incident playbook rides along.
_doc.boot_budget_tokens: The ceiling for the desk's pack (estimated read cost). The summoner checks the pack against it; over budget means trim the pack, never tolerate the overrun.
_doc.standing_constraints: Desk-specific rules that ride into every task spec for this desk, on top of your repo-wide rules.
_doc.status: summonable | standing | retired. Retired entries stay in the roster with a note; never delete the history.

roster.name: Data Pipeline Desk

scope.areas: Ingestion jobs, transforms, warehouse schemas, data-quality checks, backfills.

fare_classes.routine: lineage questions, schema lookups, backfill prep
fare_classes.priority: a pipeline read blocks a release or a stakeholder number
fare_classes.emergency: bad rows reaching production, a failed nightly load

standing_constraints: schema changes are forward-only; destructive DDL needs the operator's explicit confirmation
every reported figure cites the query or view that produced it

roster.name: Frontend Desk

scope.areas: The web client: markup, styles, client-side state, API consumption, accessibility.

fare_classes.priority: a rendering or client-state question blocks a fix
fare_classes.emergency: the client is down or showing users wrong data

standing_constraints: every displayed number traces to an API response, never a hardcoded literal
no new client dependency without a note in the task result

roster.name: Ops Desk

scope.areas: CI workflows, deploy pipeline, environments, secrets handling, schedulers, runbooks.

fare_classes.emergency: a bad deploy is live; cheapest-reversal-first applies

standing_constraints: never print or echo a secret value; reference secrets by name only
rollback is config-first: prefer the cheapest reversal before any revert commit
